title: Progress Bar
subtitle: Visual indicator of task or operation completion with support for colors, variants, labels, compact mode and indeterminate animation.

basic.title: Basic
basic.description: Simple progress bar with a numeric value from 0 to 100.

compact.title: Compact
compact.description: Allows converting the bar into a thin indicator. The value text is automatically hidden.
compact.note: Add the compact attribute to reduce the bar.

colors.title: Colors
colors.note: All NUI semantic colors are supported via the color input.

variants.title: Variants
variants.note: solid uses a neutral track with a solid fill. outline uses a transparent track with a colored border. ghost uses a track with a light alpha.

indeterminate.title: Indeterminate
indeterminate.description: Displays a looping animation when the progress is unknown.

valuePositions.title: Value Position
valuePositions.description: Controls where the value text appears.
valuePositions.note: Use the valuePosition input with values inside, top, bottom, left, right, or hidden.

valueFormat.title: Value Format
valueFormat.description: Controls the format of the value displayed with the progress bar.
valueFormat.note: Choose between percentage (e.g., 70 %) and fractional (e.g., 70 / 100) with the valueFormat input.

label.title: Label
label.description: Adds a descriptive label and controls its position.
label.note: Use the label input to add a label. Control its position with labelPosition: top, bottom, left, or right.

combined.title: Label with Value (Combined)
combined.description: Combines a descriptive label with the progress value.
combined.note: Add the showValueInLabel attribute to display the progress value inline with the label.

progress-bar-template.title: Template Slots
progress-bar-template.description: Use [nuiPbTemplate] with the slot attribute as a content child of nui-progress-bar. Use slot="value" to customize the value display, or slot="label" for the label. Both can be active simultaneously, each in its own independent position.
progress-bar-template.note: The context is identical for both slots. let-text binds to $implicit — the only binding that needs no explicit key. All others require one: let-percent reads key percent, let-v reads key value, let-max reads key max. The let-label binding is exclusive to slot="label". Local variable names are always free. See the Context Bindings table.

icons.title: Icons (trailing)
icons.description: Adds a standalone icon at the end of the progress bar row, making the status visually clear at a glance.
icons.note: Use the trailingIcon input with any RemixIcon class. The icon is always placed after the track, regardless of labelPosition.

customColors.title: Custom Colors
customColors.description: Overrides the track, fill, and text colors with custom inputs that accept any CSS color value.
customColors.note: Use the trackColor, fillColor, and textColor inputs for custom colors.

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: Divides the track into equal segments using visual dividers, ideal for communicating discrete stages such as wizard steps or onboarding flows.
steps.note: Use the steps input with a value ≥ 2. Each segment fills individually as progress advances. Customize the gap width between segments via --nui-pb-step-gap. Note: indeterminate mode ignores steps.

compact.description: Reduces the bar to a thin indicator (6 px) and hides the value.

color.description: Semantic color of the component. Falls back to global config or primary.

variant.description: Visual variant: 'solid', 'outline', 'ghost'. Falls back to global config or solid.

label.description: Descriptive label text for the progress.

trailingIcon.description: RemixIcon class rendered at the end of the bar row, independent from the label and regardless of labelPosition.

showValueInLabel.description: Displays the progress value inline with the label.

ariaLabel.description: Accessible name for screen readers when no visible label is present. Only applied if neither label nor nuiPbLabel are active.

steps.description: Visually divides the track into N segments using dividers. Requires ≥ 2 to activate.

trackColor.description: Custom background color for the track (overrides theme).

fillColor.description: Custom background color for the fill (overrides theme).

textColor.description: Custom color for the value text, label and icons.

templates.title: Content Templates
templates.description: Pass an ng-template with [nuiPbTemplate] and the slot attribute as a content child of nui-progress-bar. Both slots can be active simultaneously, each rendering in its own independent position.
templates.note: let-text (no ="..." needed) binds to $implicit — Angular’s special rule: the only context property that does not require an explicit key mapping. Every other property uses ="contextKey": let-percent="percent" reads ctx.percent, let-v="value" reads ctx.value. The local variable name (left side) is always free to choose.

value.description: Replaces the numeric value block. Compatible with any valuePosition, including inside.

label.description: Replaces the descriptive label block. Can be active simultaneously with slot="value", each in its own independent position.

context.title: Context Bindings
context.description: Template context available for both slot="value" and slot="label" (except let-label which is label-only). Syntax: let-localName="contextKey" — except $implicit, which binds with a bare let-localName and no ="...".

text.description: Pre-formatted value string (e.g. "70 %"). This is the $implicit of the context: the only binding that does not require ="...", as Angular maps it automatically with a bare let-X.

percent.description: Computed percentage: 0–100. Context key: percent. Local name is free: let-pct="percent" or let-p="percent" are equally valid.

value.description: Raw numeric value from the value input. Key: value. Local name is free: let-val="value", let-current="value", etc.

max.description: Raw maximum from the maxValue input. Context key: max.

label.description: Raw string from the label input. Exclusive to slot="label"; will be undefined in slot="value".

colors.title: Color tokens
colors.description: Local CSS custom properties resolved per color and variant by ThemeService.

trackBg.description: Track background color (resolved by theme and variant).

text.description: Text color inside the fill (inside mode).

valueText.description: Value text color outside the fill.

stepDivider.description: Color of the step divider lines (if steps are used).

sizes.title: Size tokens
sizes.description: Structural CSS custom properties that control track height, border radius and transitions. Override them on the element or a parent selector.

stepWidth.description: Width of the step divider lines, if steps has a value.

roles.title: ARIA roles & attributes
roles.description: Ensure the progress bar is accessible to assistive technology users by using appropriate ARIA roles and attributes.

role.description: The main progress bar component must have the progressbar role to indicate its function to screen readers.

valuenow.description: The aria-valuenow attribute must be dynamically updated to reflect the current progress value (0-100).

valuemin.description: The aria-valuemin attribute must be set to 0 to indicate the minimum progress value.

valuemax.description: The aria-valuemax attribute must be set to 100 to indicate the maximum progress value.

valuetext.description: If a descriptive label is provided, the aria-valuetext attribute must be updated to include both the label and the progress value for better understanding.

labelledby.description: If the progress bar has a visible label, the aria-labelledby attribute must point to the ID of the label element to associate them correctly.

setup.title: Global configuration
setup.description: The ProgressBar component supports global configuration via provideNUI(). This allows you to set default values once at the application level, avoiding repetition.
setup.note: Global configuration acts as default values and can always be overridden per instance via component inputs.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: The following properties can be configured globally via ProgressBarGlobalConfig:

rows.color: Default color of the progress bar.
rows.variant: Default variant of the progress bar.

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: NUI resolves configuration values using the following priority chain (highest priority wins):
